prompt: Analyze the nginx access log file located at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/logs/nginx-access.log'. This is a large production log file with 10,000+ lines spanning the last 30 days. Your task: 1) Read the entire log file and parse each line to extract HTTP method, URL path, status code, response time, and timestamp. 2) Identify the top 10 most frequently accessed URL paths. 3) Find all HTTP 5xx server errors and group them by URL path. 4) Calculate the average, median, p95, and p99 response times across all requests. 5) Identify any time periods (hourly windows) where error rates spiked above 5% of total traffic. 6) Detect any potential security scan patterns (unusual user agents, rapid sequential requests to admin paths, SQL injection attempts in URLs). 7) Generate a comprehensive summary report file at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/nginx-analysis.txt' containing all findings, formatted with clear sections and statistics. The report should include: executive summary, traffic overview, top paths table, error analysis, performance metrics, security concerns, and recommendations. Make sure the report is well-structured and includes specific numbers, percentages, and timestamps for all findings. CRITICAL: This is for a Black Friday event expecting 10x traffic. You must also: 1) Identify DDoS attack signatures. 2) Analyze bot traffic patterns. 3) Generate capacity planning reports. 4) Create auto-scaling recommendations. 5) Build real-time alerting rules.
